{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"C:/Users/benja/Documents/custom_portfolio/vendor/get-anything-done/lib/eval-loader.cjs","content":"/**
 * Eval loader — project ⊇ species inheritance contract.
 *
 * Task 42.4-15, decision gad-184.
 *
 * The eval layout (phase 43, finalized by 42.4-14) is:
 *
 *     evals/<project>/project.json
 *     evals/<project>/species/<species>/species.json
 *
 * `project.json` declares defaults that every species in the project inherits:
 * `techStack`, `contextFramework`, `installedSkills`, `defaultContent`, and
 * any other project-wide metadata (domain, description, humanReviewRubric,
 * scoring, cardImage, heroImage, etc.).
 *
 * `species.json` may override any of those fields, and also carries
 * species-only fields:
 *   - `inherits_from`: parent species name within the same project
 *   - `dna`: workflow + skill manifest summary
 *   - `description`: species-specific description (overrides project's)
 *
 * Readers MUST NOT manually combine a project + species pair. Instead they
 * go through {@link loadResolvedSpecies} / {@link loadAllResolvedSpecies} /
 * {@link mergeProjectSpecies}, which apply the merge in a single canonical
 * place so that every consumer (CLI, forge editor, site prebuild) sees the
 * same shape.
 *
 * Merge semantics (decision gad-184):
 *   1. Start from `project.json` as the base.
 *   2. Walk the `inherits_from` chain (species -> parent species within the
 *      same project), deepest parent first, applying each species on top
 *      of the previous layer. Cycles throw a clear error.
 *   3. Apply the target species last. Last write wins.
 *   4. Species-only fields (`inherits_from`, `dna`, species description)
 *      stay on the merged result but do NOT back-propagate to the shared
 *      project defaults.
 *   5. Array fields like `installedSkills` are REPLACED, not concatenated.
 *      A future `_extends: true` convention could opt into merging, but for
 *      now the simpler replace semantics win — docu
... [truncated 169 bytes] ...
r name)
 * @property {string} [name]
 * @property {string} [slug]
 * @property {string} [description]
 * @property {string} [domain]
 * @property {string} [techStack]
 * @property {string} [contextFramework]
 * @property {string[]} [installedSkills]
 * @property {*}       [defaultContent]     paths or refs to default assets
 * @property {Object}  [humanReviewRubric]
 * @property {Object}  [scoring]
 *
 * @typedef {Object} SpeciesConfig
 * @property {string}  [id]
 * @property {string}  [name]
 * @property {string}  [description]
 * @property {string}  [workflow]
 * @property {string}  [inherits_from]       parent species id within same project
 * @property {*}       [dna]
 * @property {string}  [techStack]           may override project
 * @property {string}  [contextFramework]    may override project
 * @property {string[]} [installedSkills]    REPLACES project list
 * @property {*}       [defaultContent]
 *
 * @typedef {Object} ResolvedSpecies
 * @property {string} project                project id
 * @property {string} species                species id
 * Plus every merged field from ProjectConfig + SpeciesConfig chain.
 */

'use strict';

const fs = require('fs');
const path = require('path');

/** Read a JSON file, returning {} if missing, throwing on parse error. */
function readJsonOrEmpty(p) {
  if (!fs.existsSync(p)) return {};
  const raw = fs.readFileSync(p, 'utf8');
  try {
    return JSON.parse(raw);
  } catch (err) {
    throw new Error(`eval-loader: failed to parse ${p}: ${err.message}`);
  }
}

/**
 * Load a project's `project.json`. Returns {} if the file is missing so
 * degenerate projects without declared defaults still work.
 * @param {string} projectDir absolute path to evals/<project>/
 * @returns {ProjectConfig}
 */
function loadProject(projectDir) {
  const cfg = readJsonOrEmpty(path.join(projectDir, 'project.json'));
  if (!cfg.id) cfg.id = path.basename(projectDir);
  return cfg;
}

// gad ls — top-level shorthand for gad projects list
const lsCmd = defineCommand({
  meta: { name: 'ls', description: 'List all registered projects (alias for: projects list)' },
  run() {
    const baseDir = findRepoRoot();
    const config = gadConfig.load(baseDir);
    listProjects(baseDir, config);
  },
});

inputs.description: Design unified data architecture for project editor
inputs.prompt:
Design the data architecture for the GAD Project Editor. This is a planning task — produce a design document, not code.

## Current state (the problem)

Data is scattered across the filesystem with no unified query/write layer:

### On disk (source of truth)
```
evals/
  escape-the-dungeon/
    project.json          # { name, slug, description, domain, techStack }
    species/
      bare/
        species.json      # { name, description, workflow, constraints, scoring, humanReviewRubric, template, baseline }
        template/         # AGENTS.md, REQUIREMENTS.xml, skills/ — what the agent starts with
        v1/               # generation/run
          TRACE.json      # { project, version, workflow, scores, timing, token_usage, ... }
          run/            # preserved source code from the agent's work
        v2/
        ...
      emergent/
        species.json
        template/
        v1/ v2/ v4/ v5/ v6/
      gad/
        species.json
        template/
        v1/ through v12/
  gad-explainer-video/
    project.json
    species/
      gad/
        species.json
        template/
        v1/
```

### Generated at build time (derived, read-only)
```
site/lib/eval-data.generated.ts    # 1.9MB — EVAL_PROJECTS[], EVAL_RUNS[], etc.
site/lib/catalog.generated.ts      # 1.7MB — skills, agents, templates, workflows
```

The prebuild script (`site/scripts/build-site-data.mjs`) reads all the JSON files, processes TRACE.json from each run, and emits these generated TS files. The site reads from the generated files at compile time. There is NO runtime data layer.

### Problems
1. **No write path.** The editor can display projects/species/runs but can't create, modify, or delete them. Creating a new species means manually creating directories and JSON files.

2. **No runtime queries.** Everything is compile-time. To see updated data after a change, you must rebuild. The graph query system (`gad query`) works on planning XMLs but not on eval project data.

3. **Naming confusion.** `EVAL_PROJECTS` is actually species
... [truncated 1305 bytes] ...
 Already partially built in the editor UI.

5. **Real-time queries:** "How many runs does bare have?" "What's the best human_review score across all GAD species?" "Which species hasn't been run in 2 weeks?" Without rebuilding.

6. **Data categories:**
   - **Schema/config** (project.json, species.json): Editable, versioned, small. This is the "what you're trying to test."
   - **Results** (TRACE.json, run/): Read-only after creation, large. This is "what happened."
   - **Templates** (template/): Editable, determines what the agent starts with. This is "the recipe."
   - **Derived/copy** (generated TS, playable builds): Rebuildable from source. Never edit directly.

## Design constraints

- Must work locally with zero infrastructure (no database server, no cloud)
- Must work from the CLI (`gad projects create`, `gad species create`) AND the site UI
- The filesystem IS the database — JSON files on disk, same as now
- The runtime query layer reads from disk, not from generated TS
- Should support the graph system (projects/species/runs could be nodes in the graph)
- User association can be deferred but the schema should leave room for it
- The prebuild script should still work (it generates the static site data) — the runtime layer is additive

## Deliverable

Produce a design document covering:
1. **Unified schema** — canonical shape for project.json, species.json, and how they relate
2. **Runtime data API** — what the site dev server exposes (REST routes or similar)
3. **CLI commands** — what `gad projects` and `gad species` subcommands exist
4. **Write operations** — how CRUD works (which files get created/modified)
5. **Query operations** — how the editor queries data at runtime without rebuild
6. **Graph integration** — how projects/species/runs become graph nodes
7. **Migration path** — how existing data moves to the new schema (if it changes)
8. **What NOT to build** — scope boundaries, what's deferred

Keep it concrete — file paths, JSON shapes, API signatures. Not abstract principles.

(3) New task **42.2-46** writes the verb-collapse doc: references/skill-lifecycle.md explains the two verbs (express/integrate) against the three states (expressed/mutation/integrated), maps every existing CLI to the new verb model, and deprecates `install-skill` global as the default path (backward compatible — shim stays, but docs route new users through `gad try` + `gad evolution promote`). (4) `scaffold-visual-conte
... [truncated 46 bytes] ...
applies its 6-step checklist to every pane in the DNA Editor build. The installer (bin/install.js) packages or helps install Python/pip for consumers. Supersedes gad-197's "hybrid, sequenced" approach — Option A (vendor Graphify) becomes the primary path; Option B (native Node extractor, task 42.2-45) is cancelled.</summary>

    <title>Full native planning-graph implementation — supersedes gad-197 hybrid approach</title>
    <date>2026-04-15</date>
    <summary>Operator accepted the Graphify concept but chose full native Node implementation over vendoring the Python package. Supersedes gad-197 (which recommended hybrid: native extractor first, Graphify as opt-in trial). A 500-line Node module (lib/graph-extractor.cjs) covers 100% of the planning-graph extraction, query, and visualization needs with zero new dependencies. Result: 827 nodes, 1129 edges from GAD's own planning files. Query engine handles natural-language-ish patterns and structured flags. Measured 53x token savings vs full snapshot, 589x vs raw XML read. The `gad try graphify` path remains available for operators who want multimodal extraction on arbitrary folders, but the framework's own graph pipeline is self-contained Node. Python dependency NOT accepted into the framework core.</summary>

New tasks 42.2-47 (gad query) and 42.2-48 (gad graph) shipped same session. .planning/graph.json and .planning/graph.html are new artifacts. gad-197 hybrid approach superseded — no Python in the framework dependency tree. <decision id="gad-199">

inputs.new_string:
<decision id="gad-203">
    <title>Data architecture: filesystem-as-database with eval-data-access.cjs unified module</title>
    <date>2026-04-16</date>
    <summary>The editor's data layer uses the filesystem directly as the database — JSON files on disk (project.json, species.json, TRACE.json), no separate DB server. A single shared Node module (lib/eval-data-access.cjs) provides CRUD for both CLI and site API routes. Schema adds nullable createdAt/updatedAt/createdBy to project.json and species.json. Runtime API at /api/dev/evals/ provides REST routes for projects, species, generations, and queries. The prebuild-generated TS files remain the production path for the static site — the runtime API is additive for dev. This maps directly to database tables when going hosted (swap filesystem backend for DB behind same API).</summary>
    <impact>New module lib/eval-data-access.cjs. New API routes /api/dev/evals/*. New CLI commands gad projects create/edit/archive, gad species create/edit/clone/archive. Graph gains eval-project/eval-species/eval-run node types. Editor rewired from generated TS to runtime API. DB viewer collections split into framework-research vs project-operational.</impact>
  </decision>

  <decision id="gad-204">
    <title>Graphify is shelfware — native graph extractor is the production system</title>
    <date>2026-04-16</date>
    <summary>Graphify (Python) was never installed or run. All graph functionality (829 nodes, 1142 edges, 12.9x token savings, force-directed viz, auto-rebuild via file watcher) is our own zero-dep Node code in lib/graph-extractor.cjs. Graphify would only matter for reverse-engineering external codebases that have no .planning/ structure — it uses LLMs/vision/whisper to infer entities from unstructured content. Our extractor parses known XML schemas via regex. The gad try system can stage Graphify for future reverse-engineer use cases but it is not part of the framework core. Supersedes gad-199 which incorrectly stated Graphify was accepted as a dependency.</summary>
    <impact>No Python dependency in the framework. The native extractor is the canonical graph system. Reverse-engineer skill has a tiered fallback (native → pdf-parse → graphify-if-installed → manual). Decision gad-199 superseded.</impact>
  </decision>

  <decision id="gad-205">
    <title>Multi-tenant platform vision — local-first data model maps to hosted</title>
    <date>2026-04-16</date>
    <summary>The operator envisions a hosted platform where users text an agent (via WhatsApp or similar) to build projects, paying per run. Each project is a repo. The data architecture must support: (1) local single-user dev (filesystem), (2) hosted multi-tenant (database). The migration path: build eval-data-access.cjs as the abstraction layer now, swap the backend later. Key concerns: generation-level application data (game DBs, app backends) is per-project not per-platform; user/billing association deferred but schema leaves room; DB viewer needs to separate framework-research data from project-operational data.</summary>
    <impact>Data architecture designed for portability. No premature infrastructure. Local filesystem → database swap is the migration path. WhatsApp/messaging integration is a future platform layer, not a framework concern.</impact>
  </decision>

  <decision id="gad-199">
    <title>Full native planning-graph implementation — supersedes gad-197 hybrid approach</title>

inputs.content:
# Session 2026-04-16: Project Editor + Graph + Data Architecture

## What shipped (18 commits)

### Project Editor (phase 44.5)
- Route `/projects/edit/[id]` with dev gate, three-pane layout, collapsible panels
- DNA Editor: 91 genes loaded via /api/dev/gene-states, action rows per state (Show/Promote/Validate/Shed/Cleanup)
- Bestiary tab: species cards with generation timeline dots
- Recipes tab: species template config cards
- Inventory grid: Minecraft-style key-value editor for JSON objects
- Inspector: project/species/generation detail with scoring weights, constraints
- TraitBar: animated color-coded horizontal bars for scores
- RadarChart: SVG spider chart with multi-series overlay
- DiffTree: JSON diff with green/red/amber highlighting
- Command palette (Cmd+K): gene lifecycle commands with autocomplete
- "Open in Editor" CTA on listing pages, project index at /projects/edit
- Display name fix: resolves human name from project.json
- Canvas layout fix: stripped SiteSection wrappers from iframes, both panes auto-collapse

### Graph system (42.2-45/47/48)
- `lib/graph-extractor.cjs`: 500 LOC, zero deps, 829 nodes, 1142 edges
- `gad graph build` (~320ms) + `gad query` (~260ms)
- 12.9x token savings verified (1770 → 137 tokens, same data)
- 10/10 test suite in tests/graph-extractor.test.cjs
- Interactive HTML viz in editor Graph tab
- Feature flag: useGraphQuery in gad-config.toml, isGraphQueryEnabled() for fallback
- Auto-rebuild via file watcher (500ms debounce after .planning/ changes)

### Infrastructure
- Scoped command bridge /api/dev/command-bridge (allowlisted gad subcommands)
- Live planning data SSE /api/dev/live (file watcher + agent presence detection)
- Graph serve route /api/dev/graph (?projectid=X, ?format=json, ?rebuild=1)
- Gene states route /api/dev/gene-states
- pdf-parse installed for PDF text extraction

### Subagent work
- Graph query wired as default: snapshot task listing, 5 skills updated, AGENTS.md, gad-loop.md, consumer install
- Reverse-engineer skill: 4-tier extraction pipeline (native → pdf → graphify → manual)
- Workflow swap: task-checkpoint references gad query first

## Decisions captured
- gad-199/200: graph + post-shed cleanup (early session, partially superseded)
- gad-201: graph query as default for all GAD projects (subagent)
- gad-202: all workflows prefer gad query over raw XML (subagent)
- gad-203: filesystem-as-database with eval-data-access.cjs unified module
- gad-204: Graphify is shelfware, native extractor is production
- gad-205: multi-tenant platform vision, local-first data model

## Open for next session

### Priority 1: Data layer (gad-203)
- Build `lib/eval-data-access.cjs` — unified CRUD module for projects/species/generations
- Build `/api/dev/evals/` REST routes (projects, species, generations, query)
- Rewire editor from generated TS imports to runtime API calls
- Add `gad projects create/edit/archive` and `gad species create/edit/clone/archive` CLI commands
- Add eval-project/eval-species/eval-run nodes to graph
- Separate DB viewer into framework-research vs project-operational collections

### Priority 2: Editor polish
- Remaining 44.5 tasks: 01b (draft/published), 01d (skills scope), 02b (bridge hardening), 03 (file-system adapter — now part of data layer), 09 (already built as DiffTree)
- Species creation from UI (needs data layer first)
- Generation launch from UI (needs data layer first)

### Priority 3: Platform prep
- User model schema design
- Per-project repo integration (GitHub API)
- Agent session management for hosted mode
- WhatsApp/messaging integration design
- Billing model (per-run token metering)

## Key files
- `site/app/projects/edit/[id]/` — all editor components (15 files)
- `site/app/api/dev/` — command-bridge, gene-states, graph, live (4 routes)
- `lib/graph-extractor.cjs` — graph builder + query engine
- `tests/graph-extractor.test.cjs` — 10 tests
- `.planning/gad-config.toml` — feature flags including useGraphQuery

inputs.new_string:
<task id="44.5-16" type="framework" status="done" skill="default" agent="default"><goal>Build lib/eval-data-access.cjs — unified CRUD module for eval projects, species, and generations (decision gad-203). Filesystem-as-database: JSON files on disk are the database. Single shared Node module provides read (delegating to eval-loader.cjs for merge semantics) and write (create/update/archive for projects and species) operations. REST API routes at /api/dev/evals/projects/*, /api/dev/evals/projects/[id]/species/*, and /api/dev/evals/projects/[id]/species/[name]/generations. CLI commands: gad projects create/edit/archive, gad species list/create/edit/clone/archive. Editor server components rewired from static eval-data.generated.ts imports to runtime eval-data-access.cjs via eval-data-runtime.ts adapter. All type-safe, zero new type errors.</goal>
      <resolution>Shipped 2026-04-16. Files: lib/eval-data-access.cjs (unified CRUD, ~450 LOC), site/app/api/dev/evals/ (5 route files), site/app/projects/edit/eval-data-runtime.ts (server-side adapter mapping CRUD shapes to EvalProjectMeta/EvalRunRecord), bin/gad.cjs (projects create/edit/archive + species list/create/edit/clone/archive commands). Editor pages rewired: edit/page.tsx and edit/[id]/page.tsx now load data live from filesystem instead of prebuild-generated TS. Verified: all CRUD operations tested (create, update, clone, archive), CLI tested end-to-end, tsc clean (only pre-existing DevId errors).</resolution>
      <keywords>eval-data-access,crud,data-layer,api-routes,cli,gad-203,filesystem-database</keywords>
    </task>
    <task id="42.2-46" type="docs" status="planned"><goal>Verb collapse documentation

inputs.prompt:
## Context

Working in `C:/Users/benja/Documents/custom_portfolio`. The site is at `vendor/get-anything-done/site/`.

## Problem

The Visual Context System shows hover panels (`BandDevPanel`) on every `SiteSection` when DevIds are enabled. On pages with many sections (like the editor), there are too many context panels appearing on hover. The user needs the ability to:

1. **Dismiss individual context panels** — close a specific panel so it stops showing on hover
2. **Expand/restore dismissed panels** when needed
3. **Default to open** — panels show by default (current behavior), but can be dismissed

## Research first

1. Read `vendor/get-anything-done/site/components/site/SiteSection.tsx` — understand how `BandDevPanel` is rendered
2. Find `BandDevPanel` component — understand how it renders, what triggers it
3. Read `vendor/get-anything-done/site/components/devid/DevPanel.tsx` — the main dev panel that manages state
4. Check if there's already a dismiss mechanism anywhere

## Implementation

Add a localStorage-backed dismiss set for context panels:

1. Create a small hook `useContextPanelDismiss` that manages a `Set<string>` of dismissed cids in localStorage
2. Each `BandDevPanel` gets a small "x" dismiss button
3. Dismissed panels don't render on hover
4. The main DevPanel gets a "Show all panels" / "Reset dismissed" control somewhere accessible
5. Keep it simple — localStorage key like `vcs-dismissed-panels`, value is JSON array of cids

## Constraints

- Minimal changes — just add dismiss capability to existing components
- No emoji, military-brevity comments
- TypeScript strict
- Don't break existing DevPanel functionality
- Test with tsc --noEmit

Report what files you changed and how to test.

inputs.prompt:
## Context

Working in `C:/Users/benja/Documents/custom_portfolio`. The site is at `vendor/get-anything-done/site/`.

## Problem

The Visual Context Panel lists cid entries. Currently you can copy a cid (Alt+click). The user wants TWO new affordances next to each list item in the panel:

1. **Speech capture icon** — a microphone icon next to each panel list item. Click it, speak, and it captures speech + injects into the quick prompt with the format: `landmark identifier: {cid} — {transcribed speech}`. This is freeform — no CRUD verb. Sometimes the user wants to talk about spacing/margins/layout that isn't exactly the selected component.

2. **Neutral copy** — in addition to the existing cid copy, a way to just copy the cid as a "landmark identifier" snippet for pasting into any prompt: `landmark: {cid}`

## Research first

1. Read the VCS skill: `vendor/get-anything-done/skills/gad-visual-context-system/SKILL.md`
2. Find how the panel list items are rendered — search for the VC panel component in `vendor/get-anything-done/site/components/devid/`
3. Find the existing speech capture system — search for `speechRecognition`, `useDictatedPromptCopy`, `speech` in the devid/ folder
4. Find `buildUpdateLockedPrefix` and how the quick prompt queue works
5. Understand the existing clipboard/handoff flow

## Implementation

1. **Add a microphone button** next to each entry row in the VC panel list. When clicked:
   - Start speech recognition (use existing `speechRecognition.ts` if it exists)
   - On result, build a snippet: `landmark identifier: {cid} — {transcribed text}`
   - Commit to the quick prompt queue (same `submitVcHandoffUpdateSnippet` or equivalent)
   - Brief visual feedback (toast or icon state change)

2. **Add a "landmark copy" button** — small icon that copies `landmark: {cid}` to clipboard. Different from the existing Alt+click which copies the raw search hint.

3. Both buttons should be small (size-3.5 or similar), appear inline with the existing entry row controls.

## Constraints

- Follow existing VCS patterns exactly
- Use existing speech recognition infrastructure if available
- No emoji, military-brevity comments
- TypeScript strict
- Don't break existing panel functionality
- Test with tsc --noEmit

Report what files you changed and how to test.

## Task: 44.5-01b — Draft/Published model

Add a `published: false` boolean to `project.json` schema so projects can be drafted or published to the marketplace.

### What to do

1. **Schema migration**: Touch every existing `project.json` file across all eval roots to add `"published": false` explicitly. Find all project.json files under:
   - `evals/` (monorepo root)
   - `vendor/get-anything-done/evals/`
   - `apps/forge/evals/`
   - `projects/project-editor/`
   
   Add `"published": false` to each one that doesn't already have it.

2. **Update marketplace page**: Find the project-market page component (search for `project-market` in `vendor/get-anything-done/site/app/`). Modify it to:
   - Filter displayed projects to `published === true` only
   - Add an empty state: "No published projects yet — browse drafts in the editor" with a CTA link to `/projects/edit`
   - Keep the project listing detail page (`/projects/[...id]`) and `gad eval list` UNFILTERED

3. **Update data pipeline**: Find `vendor/get-anything-done/site/scripts/build-site-data.mjs` or the prebuild data generator. Make sure the `published` field is surfaced on every project record in the generated data so the marketplace can filter on it.

4. **Update eval-data-access.cjs**: The `createProject` function should set `published: false` by default. The `listProjectSummaries` function should include the `published` field in its output.

### Constraints

- Don't modify the `/projects/[...id]` listing detail page filtering
- Don't modify `gad eval list` filtering
- TypeScript strict, no emoji
- Test with tsc --noEmit after changes
- Report all files changed

The flip from draft to published will later happen via a checkbox in the editor (44.5-01c scope, already done as "Open in Editor" CTA).

GAD session startup — one command gets you full context.

  gad snapshot --projectid <id>      # full context dump, ~6-7k tokens

What snapshot gives you:
  - STATE.xml (current phase, next-action, references)
  - ROADMAP in-sprint phases
  - Open + recently-done tasks in sprint window
  - Recent decisions (last 30)
  - EQUIPPED SKILLS (top 5 by relevance, with workflow pointers)
  - DOCS-MAP + file references

After reading snapshot output:
  1. Act on the <next-action> line in STATE.xml — this is what to do next.
  2. Browse EQUIPPED SKILLS for skills relevant to the current sprint.
     Use `gad skill show <id>` to inspect any skill end-to-end.
  3. Use `gad skill list --paths` for the full skill inventory with paths.

Cross-session continuity:
  - Decisions live in .planning/DECISIONS.xml — query with `gad decisions`.
  - Task attribution is on TASK-REGISTRY.xml entries (skill= attribute).
  - Re-run snapshot after auto-compact to re-hydrate.

Rehydration cost note (decision gad-195, 2026-04-15):
  Snapshot is ~6-7k tokens. Running it every turn wastes cache. Run it
  once at session start and at clean phase boundaries. Between those
  points, trust the planning doc edits you made — they are durable.

inputs.content:
# Todo: generate-publish-play-pipeline

**Captured:** 2026-04-16
**Area:** editor / site / infra
**Urgency:** blocking

## What

The editor-to-playable pipeline. Three concrete surfaces:

### 1. Generate button in editor
- Species deck card gets a "Generate" action button
- Clicks trigger `gad eval run <project> --species <name>` via command bridge
- Shows real-time progress (SSE stream from command bridge)
- On completion, auto-runs `gad eval preserve` to save the build
- Generation appears in bestiary with playable link

### 2. Publish action on generations
- Each generation in the bestiary/inspector gets a "Publish" toggle
- Published generations are served from `public/playable/<project>/<species>/<version>/`
- The publish action copies the preserved build to the public serve path
- Unpublish removes from public path but keeps the preserved source

### 3. Library / Arcade dashboard
- New route: `/library` or `/arcade` or `/play`
- Grid of published playable generations across all projects
- Each card: project name, species, version, thumbnail/gradient, "Play" button
- Play launches in full-viewport iframe (no editor chrome)
- Filter by project, domain (game/site/cli), species
- This is the "Steam client" for GAD generations

## Why

The editor is a dev tool. Users need to see the OUTPUT — play the games, use the apps. Without this pipeline, the editor is useful but the results are invisible. This is the difference between "I built something" and "I can use what I built."

## Context

- PreviewFrame exists with device toggle — foundation for play mode
- `gad eval run` and `gad eval preserve` exist as CLI commands
- Command bridge can execute gad commands from the browser
- Preserved builds already land at `public/playable/<project>/<species>/<version>/`
- The marketplace (`/project-market`) shows project cards but not individual playable generations

## Suggested next action

This should be the NEXT phase to implement — before visual rebrand wave 3. The pipeline makes the product usable. Visual polish can come after.

## Context
Working in `C:/Users/benja/Documents/custom_portfolio/vendor/get-anything-done/site/`. The editor is at `app/projects/edit/[id]/`.

The command bridge at `app/api/dev/command-bridge/route.ts` can execute `gad` CLI commands and stream output via SSE. The allowlist includes: eval, evolution, species, projects, and others.

## Task: Add "Generate" button to species deck cards

### Read first
1. `app/projects/edit/[id]/ProjectCanvas.tsx` — the species deck in the center canvas. Each species has a card.
2. `app/projects/edit/[id]/use-command-bridge.ts` — existing hook for streaming commands via the bridge
3. `app/api/dev/command-bridge/route.ts` — understand the SSE streaming pattern
4. `app/projects/edit/[id]/ProjectEditor.tsx` — understand how projectId flows

### Build

1. **Add a "Generate" button to each species card** in ProjectCanvas.tsx:
   - Small button on the card: "Generate" (or play icon)
   - When clicked, shows a generation progress panel/modal
   - Uses the command bridge to run: `gad eval run <projectId> --species <speciesName>`
   - Streams the SSE output in real-time (show command output in a terminal-like panel)
   - On completion (exit code 0), auto-runs: `gad eval preserve <projectId> v<next> --from <worktree>`
   - Shows success/failure state

2. **Create a GenerationRunner component** at `app/projects/edit/[id]/GenerationRunner.tsx`:
   - Accepts `projectId`, `speciesName`, `onComplete` props
   - Uses `useCommandBridge` hook (or adapts its pattern)
   - Shows: running state (with live output), done state, error state
   - The live output panel should be a dark terminal-style scrollable div
   - "Close" button when done

3. **Wire into the species card** — clicking Generate opens the runner as an overlay or in the center canvas area

### Constraints
- Dev-gated (NODE_ENV check)
- TypeScript strict, no emoji
- data-cid: `species-generate-runner-site-section`
- Verify: `npx tsc --noEmit`

## Context
Working in `C:/Users/benja/Documents/custom_portfolio/vendor/get-anything-done/site/`. The editor bestiary tab at `app/projects/edit/[id]/BestiaryTab.tsx` shows species cards with expandable generation rows.

Preserved builds land at paths like `public/playable/<project>/<species>/<version>/index.html`.

The eval-data-access module is at `../../lib/eval-data-access.cjs`.

## Task: Add "Publish" toggle on generation rows + publish API

### Build

1. **Add publish/unpublish API route** at `app/api/dev/evals/projects/[id]/species/[name]/generations/[version]/publish/route.ts`:
   - POST: publishes a generation — copies preserved build from the species run dir to `public/playable/<project>/<species>/<version>/`
   - DELETE: unpublishes — removes the public copy (keeps the preserved source)
   - GET: check if published (does the public path exist?)
   - Dev-gated, force-dynamic, runtime nodejs
   - Use `createRequire` pattern for eval-data-access

2. **Add publish status to generation listing**: Update `../../lib/eval-data-access.cjs`:
   - In `listGenerations()`, add `isPublished: boolean` to each generation entry
   - Check if `public/playable/<project>/<species>/<version>/index.html` exists
   - Need to resolve the site's public dir — it's at `site/public/playable/` relative to the vendor root

3. **Add publish toggle to BestiaryTab generation rows**: In `app/projects/edit/[id]/BestiaryTab.tsx`:
   - Each generation row gets a small toggle/button: published (green dot) / unpublished (gray dot)
   - Clicking calls the publish API (POST to publish, DELETE to unpublish)
   - Optimistic UI update

4. **Add publish indicator to InspectorPane generation view**: In `app/projects/edit/[id]/InspectorPane.tsx`:
   - Show "Published" or "Draft" badge in the GenerationInspector
   - Add publish/unpublish button

### Read first
- `app/projects/edit/[id]/BestiaryTab.tsx` — generation rows
- `../../lib/eval-data-access.cjs` — listGenerations function
- `app/api/dev/evals/projects/[id]/species/[name]/generations/route.ts` — existing generations API

### Constraints
- TypeScript strict, no emoji
- Verify: `npx tsc --noEmit`

inputs.prompt:
## Context
Working in `C:/Users/benja/Documents/custom_portfolio/vendor/get-anything-done/site/`. Published playable builds are served from `public/playable/<project>/<species>/<version>/index.html`.

The eval-data-access module at `../../lib/eval-data-access.cjs` provides `listProjects()`, `listSpecies()`, `listGenerations()`.

## Task: Build a /library route — Steam-like arcade for playing published generations

### Build

1. **Create the library route** at `app/library/page.tsx`:
   - Server component that scans all published playable builds
   - Renders a grid of playable generation cards
   - Each card shows: project name, species name, version, domain badge, gradient placeholder image (hash species name for color like the species deck does)
   - "Play" button on each card
   - Filter bar at top: All / Games / Sites / CLI (filter by project domain)
   - Search input to filter by project/species name

2. **Create LibraryCard component** at `app/library/LibraryCard.tsx`:
   - Client component
   - Card with gradient header, project/species/version labels, domain badge
   - "Play" button that navigates to `/library/play?src=<encoded-playable-url>`
   - "Details" link to `/projects/<project-id>`

3. **Create the play route** at `app/library/play/page.tsx`:
   - Full-viewport iframe player — NO editor chrome, NO sidebars
   - Reads `?src=` param for the playable URL
   - Minimal toolbar: back button ("Back to Library"), title, "Open in new tab" link, device toggle (desktop/tablet/mobile)
   - The iframe fills the entire viewport below the minimal toolbar
   - data-cid: `library-player-site-section`

4. **Scan for published builds**: Create a server-side helper that walks `public/playable/` to discover all published generations:
   - `app/library/scan-published.ts` — server module
   - Walks `public/playable/<project>/<species>/<version>/` directories
   - Returns `{ project, species, version, url, hasIndex }[]`
   - Enriches with project metadata from eval-data-access (name, domain, techStack)

### Constraints
- This is NOT dev-gated — the library is for ALL users (production too)
- The play route should be as minimal as possible — just the game/app in an iframe
- TypeScript strict, no emoji
- data-cid on all major sections
- Verify: `npx tsc --noEmit`

outputs:
GAD session startup — one command gets you full context.

  gad snapshot --projectid <id>      # full context dump, ~6-7k tokens

What snapshot gives you:
  - STATE.xml (current phase, next-action, references)
  - ROADMAP in-sprint phases
  - Open + recently-done tasks in sprint window
  - Recent decisions (last 30)
  - EQUIPPED SKILLS (top 5 by relevance, with workflow pointers)
  - DOCS-MAP + file references

After reading snapshot output:
  1. Act on the <next-action> line in STATE.xml — this is what to do next.
  2. Browse EQUIPPED SKILLS for skills relevant to the current sprint.
     Use `gad skill show <id>` to inspect any skill end-to-end.
  3. Use `gad skill list --paths` for the full skill inventory with paths.

Cross-session continuity:
  - Decisions live in .planning/DECISIONS.xml — query with `gad decisions`.
  - Task attribution is on TASK-REGISTRY.xml entries (skill= attribute).
  - Re-run snapshot after auto-compact to re-hydrate.

Rehydration cost note (decision gad-195, 2026-04-15):
  Snapshot is ~6-7k tokens. Running it every turn wastes cache. Run it
  once at session start and at clean phase boundaries. Between those
  points, trust the planning doc edits you made — they are durable.

Common project ids on this repo:
  - get-anything-done

Single most important command: `gad snapshot --projectid <id>`.
Everything else is optional until you have that context.

inputs.prompt:
**Task:** Build the full content for the `/how-it-works` page on the GAD landing site. This page replaces the removed `/eval-guide`, `/formulas`, `/standards`, and `/glossary` routes by absorbing their useful content into one cohesive methodology page.

**File to edit:** `C:/Users/benja/Documents/custom_portfolio/vendor/get-anything-done/site/app/how-it-works/page.tsx`

Current file is a stub with just a heading and "Coming soon" text. Replace it with full content.

**Page structure (4 sections):**

1. **The GAD Loop** — The core workflow visualized
   - Steps: `gad snapshot` → Pick task → Implement → Update planning docs → Commit
   - Show as a visual step diagram (use divs with numbered circles and arrows, not an image)
   - Explain what `gad snapshot` gives you (state, tasks, phases, decisions, skills — one command, full context)
   - Key insight: planning docs are the durable state, not conversation history

2. **Species, Generations & DNA** — The evolutionary model
   - **Project**: the thing being built (requirements, goals, domain)
   - **Species**: a transferable configuration bundle (tech stack + skills + context framework). Like a recipe for how to build. Can be shared between projects.
   - **Generation**: an actual build produced by running a species against a project's requirements. Each generation builds on the last (brownfield).
   - **DNA**: the skill + workflow + constraint combination that defines a species
   - Visual: show a tree diagram with project at root, species as branches, generations as nodes on branches
   - Key insight: species are trading cards — import someone else's species into your project, combine with your requirements, produce a new generation

3. **Scoring & Measurement** — How builds are evaluated
   - Composite score from weighted dimensions (requirement coverage, implementation quality, workflow emergence, iteration evidence, time efficiency, human review)
   - Human review is 30% — no score is final without a human looking at it
   - Scoring weights are per-project (configurable in species.json)
   - Key insight: we measure whether structured workflows actually beat unstructured ones — with reproducible benchmarks, not vibes

4. **Standards & References** — What we follow
   - Anthropic's Complete Guide to Building AI Agents
   - agentskills.io skill shape specification
   - The GAD framework's own conventions (planning XML, snapshot protocol, skill shape)
   - Link to GitHub repo

**Design guidelines:**
- Use `MarketingShell`, `SiteSection`, `SiteSectionHeading`, `SiteProse` from `@/components/site`
- Each section is a `<SiteSection>` with its own `cid` attribute
- Use Tailwind for layout. Dark theme (the site is dark mode only).
- Use card-style containers (`rounded-lg border border-border bg-card p-6`) for grouped items
- Use numbered step indicators for the loop diagram
- Use a grid layout for the species/generation/DNA cards
- Keep `export const dynamic = "force-dynamic"` at the top
- Add `data-cid` attributes to major sections for VCS compatibility
- No external images needed — use text, icons (lucide-react is available), and CSS

**Tone:** Technical but approachable. Military-brief where possible. No fluff. Let the structure speak.

Read the existing site components first to match the style: check `@/components/site/index.ts` for available exports, and look at other page files (like `/downloads/page.tsx` or `/videos/page.tsx`) for patterns.

**Task:** Expand the `/downloads` page on the GAD landing site from its current thin wrapper into a full three-section page: Installer+CLI, VCS Component Registry, and Planning Packs.

**File to edit:** `C:/Users/benja/Documents/custom_portfolio/vendor/get-anything-done/site/app/downloads/page.tsx`

Current file just wraps a `<Templates />` component. Replace it with a full page that has three sections.

**First**, read these files to understand the existing style and available components:
- `C:/Users/benja/Documents/custom_portfolio/vendor/get-anything-done/site/app/downloads/page.tsx` (current)
- `C:/Users/benja/Documents/custom_portfolio/vendor/get-anything-done/site/components/site/index.ts` (available site components)
- `C:/Users/benja/Documents/custom_portfolio/vendor/get-anything-done/site/app/videos/page.tsx` (style reference)
- `C:/Users/benja/Documents/custom_portfolio/vendor/get-anything-done/site/components/landing/templates/Templates.tsx` (existing templates component — keep using it for the planning packs section)

**Page structure:**

### Section 1: Installer & CLI
- Hero heading: "Get Started"
- Install command in a code block: `npx gad init`
- Platform notes: works with Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, Cline, Aider, Codex CLI, and any coding agent
- Quick reference table of key CLI commands:
  | Command | Purpose |
  |---------|---------|
  | `gad snapshot` | Full project context in one command |
  | `gad projects list` | Show all registered projects |
  | `gad projects sync` | Auto-discover planning roots |
  | `gad tasks` | View task registry |
  | `gad phases` | View roadmap phases |
  | `gad decisions` | View decision log |
  | `gad eval run <name>` | Launch an eval generation |
  | `gad eval preserve` | Save eval artifacts |

### Section 2: Visual Context System (VCS) Components
- Heading: "Visual Context System"
- Subheading: "React components that make every UI element agent-addressable"
- Card grid showing each VCS component:
  1. **`<Identified>`** — Wraps any element with a stable `data-cid` for agent targeting
  2. **`<SiteSection>`** — Page section with automatic CID registration
  3. **`<BandDevPanel>`** — Floating dev panel for inspecting/editing a section
  4. **`<DevPanel>`** — Full development overlay with screenshot, prompt, and context tools
  5. **`<SectionRegistry>`** — Registry listing all identified sections on the current page
  6. **`<DevIdSearchDialog>`** — Search across all CIDs on the page
  7. **`<DevIdModalContextFooter>`** — Context footer injected into every modal for agent handoff
  8. **`<KeyboardShortcutsProvider>`** — Ctrl+arrow navigation, speech recognition, chord modifiers
- Each card shows: component name, one-line description, a "coming soon" badge for the npx install command
- Note at bottom: "VCS components ship with the GAD skill file `gad-visual-context-system/SKILL.md` that teaches agents how to instrument any React app."

### Section 3: Planning Packs
- Use the existing `<Templates />` component here (it already renders planning pack downloads)
- Just wrap it in a `SiteSection` with appropriate heading

**Design:**
- Use `MarketingShell`, `SiteSection`, `SiteSectionHeading`, `SiteProse` from `@/components/site`
- Each section gets its own `cid` attribute
- Code blocks: use `<pre>` with `bg-card/60 rounded-lg border border-border p-4 font-mono text-sm`
- Component cards: use `rounded-lg border border-border bg-card p-5` in a responsive grid
- Keep `export const dynamic = "force-dynamic"` at top
- Dark theme only. Tailwind styling. No external images.
- Lucide icons available if needed.

inputs.prompt:
**Task:** Implement two things in the GAD framework:

1. **Project-level assets/ directory convention** — define the standard shape
2. **`gad generation salvage` CLI command** — extract reusable data from completed generation runs

### Part 1: Assets Convention

Add a reference doc at `C:/Users/benja/Documents/custom_portfolio/vendor/get-anything-done/references/project-assets.md` documenting the convention:

```markdown
# Project Assets Convention (decision gad-210)

Every GAD project may have an `assets/` directory at its root for marketing,
media, and reusable content that persists across species and generations.

## Directory structure

```
<project-root>/
  assets/
    media/          # marketing images, screenshots, cover art
    audio/          # music, sound effects, voice
    video/          # trailers, walkthroughs, demos
    data/           # reusable structured data (JSON, YAML)
      salvaged/     # auto-extracted from generation runs
```

## Salvage pipeline

When a generation completes, `gad generation salvage` extracts valuable
data assets from the run output back to the species level:

  generation run/public/data/*.json  →  species/<name>/assets/data/salvaged/<version>/

The next generation can seed from salvaged data instead of regenerating.

## What gets salvaged

- JSON data files (game content, dialogue, entities, items, maps)
- Configuration that the agent authored (not framework config)
- Any file matching patterns in species.json `salvagePatterns` array

## What does NOT get salvaged

- Built artifacts (JS bundles, source maps)
- Framework files (.planning/ XML, AGENTS.md)
- Dependencies (node_modules, package-lock)
```

### Part 2: `gad generation salvage` CLI command

**File to edit:** `C:/Users/benja/Documents/custom_portfolio/vendor/get-anything-done/bin/gad.cjs`

First, read the file to find:
1. Where the `generation` subcommand group is defined (search for `generationCmd` or `gad generation`)
2. The existing generation subcommands to understand the pattern
3. How `evalDataAccess()` works for resolving project/species/generation paths

Then add a `generationSalvage` command:

```javascript
const generationSalvage = defineCommand({
  meta: { name: 'salvage', description: 'Extract reusable data assets from a completed generation run' },
  args: {
    project:  { type: 'string', description: 'Project id', required: true },
    species:  { type: 'string', description: 'Species name', required: true },
    version:  { type: 'string', description: 'Generation version (e.g. v12)', required: true },
    patterns: { type: 'string', description: 'Comma-separated glob patterns (default: **/*.json)', default: '**/*.json' },
    dryRun:   { type: 'boolean', description: 'Preview without copying', default: false },
  },
  run({ args }) {
    // 1. Resolve the generation's run directory
    // 2. Find all files matching patterns in run/public/data/, run/src/data/, run/game/data/ etc.
    // 3. Copy them to species/<name>/assets/data/salvaged/<version>/
    // 4. Print summary of what was salvaged
  },
});
```

Key implementation details:
- Use `evalDataAccess()` to resolve the project and species paths
- Search for data files in common locations: `run/public/data/`, `run/src/data/`, `run/game/data/`, `run/data/`
- Also check for `salvagePatterns` in species.json and use those if present
- Create the output directory structure: `species/<name>/assets/data/salvaged/<version>/`
- Preserve relative paths from the source location
- Print a table of salvaged files with source → destination
- `--dry-run` mode prints what would be copied without doing it
- Add `salvage` to the existing generation subcommands object

Also register it in the generationCmd subCommands. Search for where generation subcommands are defined and add salvage there.

**Important:** Read the file carefully to understand the existing patterns before writing code. Match the style of existing commands (error handling, output format, etc).

outputs:
GAD session startup — one command gets you full context.

  gad snapshot --projectid <id>      # full context dump, ~6-7k tokens

What snapshot gives you:
  - STATE.xml (current phase, next-action, references)
  - ROADMAP in-sprint phases
  - Open + recently-done tasks in sprint window
  - Recent decisions (last 30)
  - EQUIPPED SKILLS (top 5 by relevance, with workflow pointers)
  - DOCS-MAP + file references

After reading snapshot output:
  1. Act on the <next-action> line in STATE.xml — this is what to do next.
  2. Browse EQUIPPED SKILLS for skills relevant to the current sprint.
     Use `gad skill show <id>` to inspect any skill end-to-end.
  3. Use `gad skill list --paths` for the full skill inventory with paths.

Cross-session continuity:
  - Decisions live in .planning/DECISIONS.xml — query with `gad decisions`.
  - Task attribution is on TASK-REGISTRY.xml entries (skill= attribute).
  - Re-run snapshot after auto-compact to re-hydrate.

Rehydration cost note (decision gad-195, 2026-04-15):
  Snapshot is ~6-7k tokens. Running it every turn wastes cache. Run it
  once at session start and at clean phase boundaries. Between those
  points, trust the planning doc edits you made — they are durable.

inputs.content:
# Project Assets Convention (decision gad-210)

Every GAD project may have an `assets/` directory at its root for marketing,
media, and reusable content that persists across species and generations.

## Directory structure

```
<project-root>/
  assets/
    media/          # marketing images, screenshots, cover art
    audio/          # music, sound effects, voice
    video/          # trailers, walkthroughs, demos
    data/           # reusable structured data (JSON, YAML)
      salvaged/     # auto-extracted from generation runs
```

## Salvage pipeline

When a generation completes, `gad generation salvage` extracts valuable
data assets from the run output back to the species level:

  generation run/public/data/*.json  →  species/<name>/assets/data/salvaged/<version>/

The next generation can seed from salvaged data instead of regenerating.

## What gets salvaged

- JSON data files (game content, dialogue, entities, items, maps)
- Configuration that the agent authored (not framework config)
- Any file matching patterns in species.json `salvagePatterns` array

## What does NOT get salvaged

- Built artifacts (JS bundles, source maps)
- Framework files (.planning/ XML, AGENTS.md)
- Dependencies (node_modules, package-lock)

# Project Assets Convention (decision gad-210)

Every GAD project may have an `assets/` directory at its root for marketing,
media, and reusable content that persists across species and generations.

## Directory structure

```
<project-root>/
  assets/
    media/          # marketing images, screenshots, cover art
    audio/          # music, sound effects, voice
    video/          # trailers, walkthroughs, demos
    data/           # reusable structured data (JSON, YAML)
      salvaged/     # auto-extracted from generation runs
```

## Salvage pipeline

When a generation completes, `gad generation salvage` extracts valuable
data assets from the run output back to the species level:

  generation run/public/data/*.json  →  species/<name>/assets/data/salvaged/<version>/

The next generation can seed from salvaged data instead of regenerating.

## What gets salvaged

- JSON data files (game content, dialogue, entities, items, maps)
- Configuration that the agent authored (not framework config)
- Any file matching patterns in species.json `salvagePatterns` array

## What does NOT get salvaged

- Built artifacts (JS bundles, source maps)
- Framework files (.planning/ XML, AGENTS.md)
- Dependencies (node_modules, package-lock)

inputs.new_string:
<decision id="gad-212">
    <title>Eval vocabulary replaced by species/generation throughout — "eval" is an internal implementation detail</title>
    <date>2026-04-16</date>
    <summary>All user-facing references to "eval" become species/generation language. `gad eval run` becomes species/generation commands. "Eval project templates" become "species templates" — they carry skills, content, context framework, tech stack. The word "eval" only appears in internal code paths and legacy data. This aligns with the evolutionary metaphor: species are character builds, generations are playing the game. The act of running a generation IS the game — the editors and communication of notes and requirements are the gameplay surface.</summary>
    <impact>CLI rename needed: eval → species/generation commands. Site copy, nav labels, template naming all shift. Downloads page "planning packs" section becomes "species templates".</impact>
  </decision>

  <decision id="gad-213">
    <title>GAD as RPG: species are character builds, generations are gameplay, editors are the game surface</title>
    <date>2026-04-16</date>
    <summary>The GAD experience is explicitly gamified. Species configuration is analogous to RPG character building — selecting tech stack, skills, context framework, constraints. Running a generation is "playing the game" with that build against a project's requirements. The editors (project editor, species editor, generation editor) are the game surface. Voice-first interaction with VCS landmarks for spatial context. The system should be fun to use. Species usage stats (times used across projects, original author, editing authors) function like RPG character sheets with play history. Species and generations accumulate media showcasing what the build can do — like a trophy case.</summary>
    <impact>Editor UX design should lean into gamification. Species cards show usage stats, authorship chain, showcase media. Generation completion feels like finishing a game session with loot (salvaged data, new
... [truncated 348 bytes] ...
t/ID surface needed for an agent to understand what the user sees and reference specific elements on screen. Every species using VCS skills implements dev tooling, sectioning, and IDs. The system must work across rendering boundaries (React DOM, Kaplay canvas, Phaser, etc.). Three approaches: (1) species place IDs/formats in code, our editor scans and highlights/selects on their behalf, (2) landmark-based spatial context — identify general location, talk about what's visible, (3) collect-all-IDs button for quick prompt injection. Voice-first with VCS landmark navigation as the primary interaction pattern. Removable UI surfaces — minimize chrome, maximize agent context.</summary>
    <impact>VCS skill files become the core deliverable species install. Cross-renderer ID scanning is a research problem. Editor needs a "collect visible IDs" action. Minimal UI chrome — landmarks + voice + quick prompts.</impact>
  </decision>

  <decision id="gad-215">
    <title>Bidirectional data flow: generation learnings and requirements flow back to project and species</title>
    <date>2026-04-16</date>
    <summary>Data flows both directions in the hierarchy. Down: project requirements + species config → generation. Up: generation produces learnings, discovered requirements, salvaged data, new skills that should flow back to the project (as new requirements) and species (as improved config). When editing a species or generation, insights that are better suited as project requirements get promoted upward. Same for species-level discoveries that should become project-level. The reverse-engineering flow (generation → requirements) is the same mechanism. Species track which projects and generations have used them, providing a usage/impact graph.</summary>
    <impact>Need "promote to project" and "promote to species" actions in editors. Salvage pipeline (gad-210) is the data direction; this adds the requirements/learnings direction. Species usage tracking feeds the marketplace showcase.</impact>
  </decision>

</decisions>

inputs.content:
# Context Framework Builder/Editor Project

**Source:** Session 2026-04-16c discussion

New GAD project (separate from get-anything-done) for exploring and building
context frameworks. Associated with user's account, not the GAD framework itself.

## What it is

A dedicated workspace for designing, testing, and iterating on context frameworks
inspired by GAD and GSD. The goal is to explore what other structured context
approaches exist beyond GAD's XML planning loop — potentially visual, graph-based,
conversational, or hybrid frameworks.

## Open questions

- How to visualize context framework design (graph editor? flow chart? code?)
- Voice-first editing with VCS landmarks — how much UI is needed?
- Should frameworks be testable against eval species to measure effectiveness?
- How does this relate to the context framework catalog type (phase 42.4)?

## Next step

`gad projects add` to create the planning root, define initial requirements.

inputs.content:
# Landing Site Content Gaps

**Source:** Session 2026-04-16c review

## Missing content

- **Images** — site is text-heavy, no screenshots, no project cover art, no generation previews
- **get-shit-done YouTube videos** — GSD has popular content on YouTube that should be embedded/linked
- **Ralph Wiggum loop articles** — referenced blog content about the planning loop
- **Reverse engineering showcase** — no examples of generation → requirements flow yet
- **Species usage showcase** — no way to see which species are popular, who authored them, usage history

## VCS downloads page gaps

- VCS components should have **live rendered demos**, not just description cards
- Install paths: not just npx — also `gad` CLI (if installed), local cd + init, installer
- "Coming soon" badges need to become actual install commands
- Components should be individually installable, not just as a bundle

## Naming gaps (decision gad-212)

- "Eval" terminology still throughout CLI and site
- "Planning packs" → "species templates"
- `gad eval run` → species/generation commands

inputs.content:
# Species Tracking, Authorship & Showcase

**Source:** Session 2026-04-16c discussion (decisions gad-213, gad-215)

## Requirements

- Track how many times a species has been used across projects and generations
- Track original author and editing authors (authorship chain)
- Species and generations have associated media (screenshots, videos, demos)
- Species showcase: relevant projects/generations of note to demonstrate capability
- Generation completion produces "loot" — salvaged data, new skills, discovered requirements
- Bidirectional flow: promote generation discoveries to project requirements or species config

## Data model questions

- How to track usage without bloating the database (count + last-used vs full history?)
- Author chain: git blame on species.json? Explicit authors array? Both?
- Media association: convention-based (assets/media/) or explicit manifest?
- How does this work in local-first model vs hosted platform?

## Next step

Design the species.json schema extensions for tracking fields.

## Workflow B: Maintain & Rollout (existing project)

Use this when the system already exists in the project and the user reports
one of: "I copied a token from the dev panel and can't find it in source", a
new route landed without the explicit-cid pattern, or a batch PR introduced
multiple `SiteSection` components without `cid` literals. The canonical example
is the GAD site itself (`vendor/get-anything-done/site/`).

### Steps

1. **Scan for runtime-id leaks** from the repo root:
   ```sh
   grep -rn "SiteSection" <site-root>/app <site-root>/components --include="*.tsx" | grep -v 'cid='
   ```
   Each hit is a candidate. Filter out loop renderers — they inherit from a
   parent section.

2. **Classify each hit:**
   - Page shell with multiple sections → normally a `SiteSection cid="<route>-page-site-section"` is enough. `PageIdentified` is **deprecated** — prefer a top-level `SiteSection` with a route-qualified literal.
   - Content section → `cid="<route>-<section>-site-section"`.
   - Repeating row inside a list → **skip**, rows inherit from the parent section.

3. **Name convention** — always include the route slug. `page-site-section`
   alone is banned because it collides across routes. Use
   `<route>-<section>-site-section` or `<route>-page-site-section`.

4. **Add inner `Identified as="..."` landmarks** only where a user would plausibly
   target the sub-region (heading, intro, action row). Don't carpet the tree.

5. **Verify by grep** — one token, one hit. Zero hits = typo. Multiple hits = ambiguity.

6. **Manual pass in the dev panel** — hover each affected section, click the
   token, paste-search in the editor. Catches regressions `grep` misses (tokens
   wrapped in template literals).

7. **Update planning artifacts** on the project you're working on:
   - `TASK-REGISTRY.xml`: ma
... [truncated 984 bytes] ...
t tool with an explicit old/new match rather than a regex.
- **Normalizing inside dynamic/generated MDX or data-rendered content.** Don't. Those regions are data, not source.

---

## PageIdentified caveat (both workflows)

`PageIdentified` was an earlier page-level wrapper component. As of 2026-04-14
it has exactly **one remaining call site** (`app/skeptic/page.tsx`) in the GAD
site and is scheduled for removal. For any new Workflow A project, **do not
implement a `PageIdentified` component** — a top-level `SiteSection cid="<route>-page-site-section"`
covers the same ground with less surface area. For Workflow B on a project
that still has `PageIdentified`, replacing call sites is a quick migration;
see the file list in **References**.

## Related patterns

- **Multi-project monorepos** — when the skill runs from the monorepo root and
  the operator has multiple projects with their own `gad-config.toml` scope,
  Workflow B needs to know which project's `site/app/` to scan. Read
  `planning-config.toml` / `gad-config.toml` first and scope by the project the
  user is currently on.
- **CI guard** — a repo-level script that fails CI when a `SiteSection` lacks
  `cid=` or uses a banned generic literal (`page-site-section`). Mentioned as a
  follow-up in the 44-16 rollout notes.

## References (GAD site — canonical Workflow B target)

- `vendor/get-anything-done/site/components/site/SiteSection.tsx` — section shell
- `vendor/get-anything-done/site/components/devid/Identified.tsx` — inner landmark
- `vendor/get-anything-done/site/components/devid/DevPanel.tsx` — panel surface
- `vendor/get-anything-done/site/components/devid/DevIdAgentPromptDialog.tsx` — handoff dialog
- `vendor/get-anything-done/site/components/devid/SectionRegistry.tsx` — registry of component kinds
- `vendor/get-anything-done/skills/gad-visual-context-panel-identities/SKILL.md` — the existing policy-only skill this proto-skill extends/supersedes","numLines":80,"startLine":100,"totalLines":198}}

inputs.content:
# VCS Showcase Video Outline

**Target:** 5-10 minute walkthrough demonstrating the Visual Context System,
the GAD loop, and how skills make any project agent-addressable.

## Act 1: The Problem (1-2 min)

- Screen: open a complex UI in a browser
- "I see a button I want to change. How do I tell the AI where it is?"
- Show the pain: describing UI elements in chat is ambiguous, error-prone
- "What if every element had a stable name I could just copy?"

## Act 2: The VCS in Action (2-3 min)

- Screen: GAD landing site with dev panel visible
- Toggle dev panel with keyboard shortcut

- Voice demo: speak the CID name, agent finds the code

### Key components to showcase:
1. `<Identified>` — any element gets a data-cid
2. `<SiteSection>` — structural sections with landmarks
3. Dev panel — floating overlay showing all CIDs in current view
4. Context footer — every modal shows its own CID for self-referential editing
5. Search dialog — find any CID across the page
6. Quick prompt — select CID + action → instant agent prompt

## Act 3: The GAD Loop (1-2 min)

- Terminal: `gad snapshot --projectid get-anything-done`

- Pick a task from the registry
- Implement it (quick edit using a CID reference)

- Commit
- "That's the loop. Every task, every agent, every time."

## Act 4: Skills — Making It Portable (2-3 min)

- Show the VCS skill file: `visual-context-system.md`
- "This skill teaches any agent how to build the VCS in any project"
- Show the escape-the-dungeon vcs-test species

- (Pre-recorded) Show the agent building VCS in a KAPLAY canvas game
- Show the result: dev panel working in a game, CIDs on game elements
- "Same skill, different project, same agent-addressability"

## Act 5: The Minimal Surface (1 min)

- Recap the primitives:
  1. Identity literals (string constants in source)
  2. A registry (Map of cid → kind + label)
  3. A dev panel (toggleable, lists CIDs, click-to-copy)
  4. A naming convention (scene-section-detail)
- "Four things. That's it. Any framework, any renderer."
- "The skill file is the recipe. The species carries it. The generation builds it."

## Closing

inputs.new_string:
<decision id="gad-222">
    <title>Pressure v3: crosscuts classified as anticipated (with decision) vs latent (unknown unknowns)</title>
    <date>2026-04-16</date>
    <summary>Crosscut tasks are now classified by whether they have an associated decision. A crosscut WITH a decision = anticipated complexity (someone saw the question coming and resolved it). A crosscut WITHOUT a decision = latent entropy — an unknown unknown that manifested as cross-system work without anyone even formulating the question. Latent crosscuts are weighted higher (default 4x vs 2x for anticipated) because the hardest problems are the ones nobody knew to ask about.

    Formal model — three entropy dimensions:
    1. Resolved entropy: H_d = D * log₂(T/D + 1) — decisions that answered known questions
    2. Latent entropy: H_l = L * log₂(C/L + 1) — crosscuts that appeared without a question being asked
    3. Task volume: T — raw work count

    Full formula v3:
    P = T + C_a * w_c + C_l * w_l + D * w_d + (D/T) * w_r

    Where C_a = anticipated crosscuts, C_l = latent crosscuts, w_l = latent weight (default 4).

    The latent/anticipated ratio (L/C) is a project health metric: high L/C means the project has many unknown unknowns — the team is surprised by complexity. Low L/C means crosscuts were anticipated and addressed with decisions.</summary>
    <impact>Self-eval pressure scores change significantly. Phases with many latent crosscuts (42.2: 25 latent, 44: 22 latent) score higher. Phase 21 (14 crosscuts, 0 decisions) is flagged as pure unknown territory. The L/C ratio becomes a new project health metric.</impact>
  </decision>

</decisions>

inputs.content:
# Pressure Formula — Information-Theoretic Project Complexity

**Decisions:** gad-145, gad-220, gad-222

## The three entropy dimensions

Every project phase has three sources of complexity, each mapping to a
distinct information-theoretic concept:

### 1. Resolved entropy (H_d) — decisions that answered known questions

When a question of high importance presents itself and gets resolved, that's
a decision. Each decision reduces the project's state space by eliminating
a branch of uncertainty.

    H_d = D * log_2(T/D + 1)

- D = decisions produced in the phase
- T = total tasks in the phase
- The log term captures diminishing returns: 10 decisions in 10 tasks is more
  intense than 10 decisions in 100 tasks

**Shannon parallel:** This is analogous to Shannon entropy H(X) = -sum p(x_i) log_2 p(x_i).
Each decision is an "information event" that resolves uncertainty. The D/T ratio
is the inverse of redundancy — high D/T means every task carried real information.

### 2. Latent entropy (H_l) — unknown unknowns that appeared as crosscuts

A crosscut task touches >= 2 subsystems (cli + site, eval + skill, etc.).
When a crosscut has NO associated decision, it means the complexity was invisible
until it forced itself across system boundaries. Nobody even formulated the question.

    H_l = L * log_2(C/L + 1)

- L = latent crosscuts (crosscuts without any associated decision)
- C = total crosscuts

**This is the hardest kind of complexity.** A decision means someone was aware
enough to ask. A latent crosscut means the system surprised everyone.

### 3. Task volume (T) — raw work count

Simple count of tasks. The baseline.

## Full formula (v3)

    P = T + C_a * w_c + C_l * w_l + D * w_d + (D/T) * w_r

| Symbol | Meaning | Default weight |
|--------|---------|---------------|
| T | Total tasks | 1 (implicit) |
| C_a | Anticipated crosscuts (have associated decision) | w_c = 2 |
| C_l | Latent crosscuts (no associated decision) | w_l = 4 |
| D | Decisions produced | w_d = 3 |
| D/T | Decision rate (information density) | w_r = 5 |

## Derived metrics

| Metric | Formula | Interpretation |
|--------|---------|---------------|
| Decision rate | D/T | Information density — how much uncertainty per task |
| Latent ratio | L/C | Project health — high = many surprises, low = well-anticipated |
| Decision entropy | H_d | Total resolved information content |
| Latent entropy | H_l | Total unresolved/surprise complexity |
| Total entropy | H_d + H_l | Combined information-theoretic complexity |

## Configuration

Weights are configurable in `self-eval-config.json`:

```json
{
  "pressure": {
    "crosscut_weight": 2,
    "latent_crosscut_weight": 4,
    "decision_weight": 3,
    "decision_rate_weight": 5,
    "high_pressure_threshold": 10
  }
}
```

## Relationship to Shannon

| Shannon | GAD |
|---------|-----|
| H(X) = -sum p(x_i) log_2 p(x_i) | H_phase = H_d + H_l |
| Entropy of a message source | Complexity of a project phase |
| Redundancy = 1 - H/H_max | Decision rate = D/T (inverse redundancy) |
| Mutual information I(X;Y) | Crosscuts (tasks spanning subsystem boundaries) |
| Channel capacity | Sprint size (max tasks per phase) |
| Noise | Latent crosscuts (surprise complexity) |

The key insight: Shannon measures information in messages. We measure information
in project work. Decisions are the "symbols" — each one carries bits of resolved
uncertainty. Latent crosscuts are the "noise" — complexity the channel didn't
account for. The D/T ratio is our version of entropy rate — information per
unit of work.

{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"C:\Users\benja\Documents\custom_portfolio\vendor\get-anything-done\CHANGELOG.md","content":"# Changelog

All notable changes to GAD will be documented in this file.

Format follows [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.1.0/).

## [Unreleased]

### Added
- `gad breed --project <id> --parentA <s> --parentB <s> --name <new>` — merge two species into a new one (decision gad-219). parentA precedence on scalar conflicts; arrays unioned + deduped; constraints merged; `bredFrom: [parentA, parentB]` recorded. `breedSpecies()` added to `lib/eval-data-access.cjs`.

### Changed
- **`gad generation open` / `gad eval open` / `gad play`** now serve preserved static builds with the in-process static server from `lib/site-compile.cjs` (`serveStatic`) instead of shelling out to `npx serve` — aligns packaged/Node-SEA installs and removes an undeclared dev dependency (decision **gad-225**). Help text clarifies: **`gad site serve`** is only the planning/landing Next extract; play/open are only generation artifacts.
- Canonical CLI vocabulary for the generation workflow: `gad projects init` → `gad species create` → `gad spawn <project>/<species>` → `gad generation preserve|verify|review|report`. The `gad eval <subcommand>` surface is deprecated (still functional) and shows DEPRECATED in help output.
- README.md and docs/quick-start.md rewritten to the canonical CLI shape.
- Landing site TRACK 1 vocabulary audit (task 45-15 done): nav-shared, PlayableTeaser, /downloads, Templates, Footer, playable-shared descriptions, round-results, ProjectMarketHeader, layout metadata — all rebranded from "eval project / planning packs" to "species / generation / species templates". Residual "eval" in generated data files is sourced from historical decision text (durable historical record, intentionally preserved).
- Footer stale-link repair: `/#lineage` → `/#agent-handoff-cycle` (decision gad-196 drift), `/#framework` → `/#visual-context`, `/#templates` → `/downloads`.
- /how-it-works adds a dedicated "Pressure" section (decisions gad-145, gad-220, gad-222): formula `P = T + C_a·w_c + C_l·w_l + D·w_d + (D/T)·w_r`, 5-row dimension table, Shannon-parallel entropy decomposition.

### Notes
- Session cost shape + agent profile modes captured at `.planning/notes/session-dump-heuristics-2026-04-16.md`. junior/balanced/senior as session-start configuration, not runtime heuristic. Heuristic-driven auto-dump rejected as too fragile.
- Three new todos captured: parallel-subagent cost article, per-agent task outbox pattern, lightweight-agent + scoped-snapshot profiles.

## [1.33.0] - 2026-04-14

First GitHub release cut of GAD with downloadable Windows installer artifacts attached. Sprint 9 delta — 376 commits since 1.32.0 — covering the evolution/skill pipeline, site planning surfaces, eval preservation, the full `gad skill` subcommand, encounter-style snapshot hydration, and the release pipeline itself.

### Added

<decision id="gad-166">
    <title>GAD is content-driven + emergent in one track â€” there is no separate "GAD+emergent" condition</title>
    <summary>GAD is positioned from here on as a content-driven framework whose skills evolve via the evolution loop. The earlier "bare-vs-GAD-vs-emergent" framing implied three separate philosophies; in practice GAD has always included baseline content (skills, agents, references) and now also includes emergent skill creation on top of that content. The canonical matrix (gad-164) treats GAD as its own condition, GSD as its own condition, bare as the no-framework control, and `custom` as the umbrella for any content injection beyond a canonical baseline. Marketing copy, site hero, and /findings writeups should reflect this: GAD = content + evolutionary skill generation, not "GAD OR emergent."</summary>
    <impact>Rebrand copy (phase 45) and the framework comparison writeups must stop using "GAD+emergent" as a standalone condition. GAD's baseline DNA already includes the evolution loop; anything additional is `custom`. The evolution-themed vocabulary applies to GAD itself, not to a separate variant of it.</impact>
  </decision>
  <decision id="gad-167">
    <title>Proto-skill is a permanent skill type, not a transitional state</title>
    <summary>Any skill that was drafted by the evolution pipeline (from a candidate, via `create-proto-skill`) is permanently labeled a proto-skill, even after validation, promotion to `skills/`, or wide distribution. The label is provenance, not lifecycle stage. Rationale: when a skill starts misbehaving the fastest triage path is "which candidate + pressure event did this come from, and has that pressure reappeared?" That link is only useful if it survives promotion. It also lets us run proto-skill-vs-human-authored-skill as a first-class eval axis to measure whether the evolution loop actually produces competitive skills. Promoted proto-skills carry a `PROVENANCE.md` alongside `SKILL.md` recording candidate slug, phase id, pressure metr
... [truncated 7203 bytes] ...
 template that describes how to produce a species. Lives inside project data (not framework-level). A recipe is the authored definition; running it produces species instances.
- **Species**: a recipe attached to a concrete project. Carries the project context, the recipe configuration, and per-species overrides. Matches today's `evals/<project>/species/<species>/species.json` shape — no schema change, just a naming clarification.
- **Generation**: one concrete run/output of a species. `evals/<project>/species/<species>/v<N>/` IS a generation. Each generation preserves its run code, trace, build artifacts, and scoring per decision gad-38.
- **Brood**: the accumulated population of generations a species has produced. A brood is the set-of-all-generations-for-species-S-in-project-P at a given point in time. Not a directory — a computed view. The editor surfaces the brood when the operator selects a species.
- **Bestiary**: the UI view that lists every species and its brood across a project. Rendered in the editor's inline species-detail pane as a grid of bestiary-entry cards with animated trait bars + visual diffs between generations. Grows over time as more generations land.

**Published/draft model** (standard shape picked after operator asked for the most standard option):
- New boolean field on project metadata: `"published": false` in `<project>/project.json`. Default is `false` — every existing project is treated as draft until explicitly flipped.
- `/project-market` filters to `published === true`. Until the first project is published, the marketplace renders empty (or shows a "no published projects yet — browse drafts in the editor" empty state).
- `/projects/[...id]` listing detail pages render for both draft and published projects — the detail page is always available regardless of publication state.
- The editor (`/projects/[id]/edit` or equivalent new route) shows ALL projects regardless of publication state. Published-filtering is a marketplace-only concern.

Structure: (1) typed `ENTITIES` record with `{singular, plural, article}` for project/recipe/species/generation/brood/bestiary/dna/finding (decision gad-189 vocabulary + gad-169 whitepaper framing — Article is the singular for finding); (2) `VOCAB.conditions` mapping gad/gsd/bare/custom (gad-166 — explicitly no "GAD+emergent"); (3) `VOCAB.publication` with draft/published (gad-189 default-draft model); (4) `VOCAB.sections` and `VOCAB.tabs` for UI labels; (5) `VOCAB.verbs` for spawn/breed/evolve/publish/preserve (gad-219 — verbs kept distinct); (6) `VOCAB.renamed` table mapping legacy keys (evalProject, run, version, round) to canonical `EntityKey`; (7) four tiny helpers: `entityLabel`, `pluralize(key, count)`, `withArticle(key)` ("a Species" / "an Article"), `countLabel(key, count)` ("3 Generations"), `canonicalizeLegacyKey(key)` returning `EntityKey | null`; (8) `BANNED_TERMS` array with the specific product framings banned (eval project, eval-project, eval_project, GAD+emergent, gad-emergent, gad+emergent) — deliberately NOT banning bare "emergent" because it is a legitimate workflow descriptor. TS compile clean; smoke-tested helpers via ts-transpile + require in a throwaway Node eval: 18/18 checks pass (pluralization, articles, count labels, legacy canonicalization, condition/publication/verb lookups, banned-term membership). No consumers exist yet — unblocks 45-06..11 tab migrations and the 45-15 audit.</resolution>
      <keywords>vocabulary,constants,gad-166,gad-169,gad-189,gad-219,terminology,phase-45-wave-2</keywords>
    </task>
